displayTitle: Storage Is a Regulated State, Not a Pause

beats.narration: Most stolen radiography sources leave in a stolen truck, overnight or at lunch. Between jobs, a sealed source is never just parked.

payload.title: STORAGE IS A REGULATED STATE
payload.sub: Not in storage means surveilled. In storage means secured.

beats.narration: Part 20 allows exactly two states, and you must always know which. Eyes on it, or locked away. There is no in-between.

tiles.s: A tailgate at lunch is neither

beats.narration: Every arrangement starts the same. Survey it shielded, lock it and pull the key, then log the move and count it quarterly.

chips.s: 34.23 — key in the lock is unlocked
34.71 — dates out of and into storage

beats.narration: Above twenty-one curies of iridium, Part 37 stacks on. The truck needs two independent barriers and a disabled vehicle. Pulling the ignition key does not count.

tiles.s: 37.53(a) — locked device in a bolted box

beats.narration: Vaults trade mobility for neighbors: shield to the public limits, lock it, post Caution Radioactive. Survey it, and prove every reading holds.

payload.sub: Constant surveillance means eyes on, able to intervene — not a glance through the diner window.
